protocols.Paper_title: Mammalian reovirus, a nonfusogenic nonenveloped virus, forms size-selective pores in a model membrane
protocols.Evidence: RBC ghosts were made by hypotonic lysis, washed, pelleted, resealed, and later used as RG preparations.

protocols.Paper_title: Mammalian reovirus, a nonfusogenic nonenveloped virus, forms size-selective pores in a model membrane
protocols.Evidence: Hemolysis reactions were chilled and centrifuged; a detergent reaction containing Triton X-100 served as the complete-lysis reference.

protocols.Paper_title: Mammalian reovirus, a nonfusogenic nonenveloped virus, forms size-selective pores in a model membrane
protocols.Evidence: Hemolysis-like reactions with RGs were chilled, diluted, and analyzed by flow cytometry; microscopy was also used for RG leakage.

protocols.Paper_title: Mammalian reovirus, a nonfusogenic nonenveloped virus, forms size-selective pores in a model membrane
protocols.Evidence: RG leakage reactions were fractionated on gradients, tubes were washed and boiled, and fractions were analyzed by SDS/PAGE or counting.
